description: Hand-written seed corpus for the deterministic retrieval workbench. Documents mirror early Sanity-shaped Content Entities and gold-set Parent Prompts.

documents.title: Allergy and medical safety
documents.contentMap: Parent concern about whether camp can safely support allergies, medication, health incidents, nurse access, and emergency escalation.

documents.title: Homesickness and child readiness
documents.contentMap: Parent concern about whether a child is emotionally ready for separation, overnight routines, independence, and homesickness support.

documents.title: Transportation logistics
documents.contentMap: Parent concern about bus routes, drop-off, pick-up, travel supervision, luggage handling, and arrival timing.

documents.title: Packing and equipment
documents.contentMap: Parent concern about what a child should bring, what is prohibited, laundry, labels, bedding, electronics, and forgotten items.

documents.title: Sibling attendance and grouping
documents.contentMap: Parent concern about siblings attending together, cabin grouping, age differences, and whether siblings can see each other.

documents.title: Pricing and affordability
documents.contentMap: Parent concern about tuition, deposits, payment timing, refunds, optional costs, and whether camp fits the family budget.

documents.title: Swimming and water safety
documents.contentMap: Parent concern about swim ability, waterfront supervision, lifejackets, swim tests, boating, and water emergency protocols.

documents.title: Health Centre and medication policy
documents.contentMap: Explains Health Centre staffing, medication storage, daily medication administration, parent notifications, and when off-site medical care is used.

documents.title: Anaphylaxis and severe allergy response protocol
documents.contentMap: Step-by-step protocol for severe allergy prevention, Epipen access, staff response, emergency services contact, and parent communication.

documents.title: Medication check-in procedure
documents.contentMap: Describes how families hand in medication at arrival, required labels, dosage confirmation, Health Centre logging, and secure storage.

documents.title: Staff medical awareness training
documents.contentMap: Outlines pre-camp staff training for allergy awareness, medication privacy, incident reporting, and knowing when to involve medical staff.

documents.title: First-time overnight readiness guide
documents.contentMap: Helps parents assess Child Readiness for overnight camp with practice sleepovers, separation confidence, self-care routines, and communication expectations.

documents.title: Homesickness support procedure
documents.contentMap: Explains how cabin staff notice homesickness, provide reassurance, keep children engaged, escalate concerns, and communicate with parents when needed.

documents.title: Parent story about a nervous first-time child
documents.contentMap: A parent describes sending a nervous first-time child, the first letters home, staff support, and the child's growing confidence by pickup.

documents.title: Packing checklist
documents.contentMap: Lists required clothing, bedding, footwear, toiletries, rain gear, labels, laundry bag, and optional comfort items for camp.

documents.title: Electronics and phone rule
documents.contentMap: States which electronics are not allowed at camp, why phones stay home, and how parents can contact the camp office.

documents.title: Toronto bus route
documents.contentMap: Provides the Toronto bus departure location, check-in timing, luggage expectations, travel supervision, and pickup process.

documents.title: Airport pickup route
documents.contentMap: Explains airport pickup coordination, flight details required from parents, staff greeting procedure, and supervised transfer to camp.

documents.title: Waterfront and swimming program
documents.contentMap: Describes swim periods, lake activities, swim ability grouping, boating options, supervision expectations, and waterfront participation.

documents.title: Waterfront safety protocol
documents.contentMap: Defines waterfront supervision, swim checks, buddy system, lifejacket rules, boating boundaries, and emergency response at the lake.

documents.title: Junior overnight session
documents.contentMap: Session for younger children with dates, age range, cabin support, activity mix, pricing, and availability fields.

documents.title: Two-week classic session
documents.contentMap: Two-week session with dates, age range, activity breadth, sibling attendance options, pricing, deposits, and availability fields.

documents.title: Pricing, deposit, and refund policy
documents.contentMap: Explains tuition, required deposit, payment schedule, refund dates, optional charges, and what is included in the camp fee.

documents.title: Cabin assignment procedure
documents.contentMap: Explains how cabin groups are assigned by age, session, friendship requests, sibling considerations, and social fit.

documents.title: Cabin social fit
documents.contentMap: Parent concern about cabin assignments, friendship requests, social comfort, peer age, and whether a child will feel included.

documents.title: Health form requirement
documents.contentMap: States that parents must submit health history, medication details, allergy notes, emergency contacts, and physician instructions before arrival.

documents.title: Cabins are grouped by age and social fit
documents.contentMap: Trust-relevant claim that cabin assignments consider age, social fit, friendship requests, and staff judgment rather than only registration order.

goldSet.prompt: My child has a peanut allergy and carries an Epipen. How would camp keep them safe?
Can staff make sure my son takes his daily medication at the right time?
My daughter has never slept away from home and gets homesick. Is overnight camp too much?
If phones are not allowed, how do I know my nervous child is doing okay?
We live in Toronto. How does the bus pickup work and who supervises the trip?
Can my child fly in alone, and will someone meet them at the airport?
What exactly should we pack for a two-week session, and are electronics allowed?
Can my two children attend the same session and be placed near each other?
How much does camp cost, what is due as a deposit, and what happens if we cancel?
My child is not a strong swimmer. Can they still join lake activities safely?
